pair_convention.spot_explanation: CAD/JPY at 114.57 means 1 Canadian Dollar buys 114.57 Japanese Yen. A rising pair rate indicates CAD is strengthening against JPY (each CAD buys more JPY); a falling pair rate indicates CAD is weakening against JPY (each CAD buys fewer JPY). The pair is quoted as units of JPY per 1 CAD.
pair_convention.bearish_means: pair FALLS (CAD weakens vs JPY / JPY strengthens vs CAD)

executive_summary: CAD/JPY closed at 114.57 on May 9, 2026, sitting at the 82nd percentile of its 52-week range (101.25–117.49) after rallying approximately 10% from the April 2025 lows near 101.25 but pulling back 2.5% from the late-April 2026 peak at 117.49. The dominant bilateral narrative is a collision between oil's double-edged effect — Canada exports it (CAD-positive), Japan imports it (JPY-negative) — and a hawkish BOJ that is actively closing the policy-rate gap through threatened June/July hikes and demonstrated ¥5.4 trillion intervention on May 1. Over one month the pair has declined 1.1%, reflecting BOJ intervention and hawkish rhetoric overwhelming the carry and oil tailwinds that had driven the rally from 101.25 to 117.49 over the prior year. The BoC-BOJ policy rate spread of +150bps generates attractive carry for long positions, but carry-to-vol has compressed sharply as JPY implied volatility has risen post-intervention. The pair faces a binary regime: if Iran ceasefire firms and oil normalises toward $85, the oil-driven CAD/JPY upside collapses as both legs lose their tailwind — JPY strengthens on improved terms of trade while CAD's carry advantage isn't enough to compensate. Our headline stance is Bearish CAD/JPY tactically (conviction 2/5, 55% confidence) and Bearish CAD/JPY strategically (conviction 3/5, 62% confidence), targeting 110–112 over 1–6 months on BOJ normalisation, intervention asymmetry, and the pair's sensitivity to any oil/risk-off shock that compresses both legs simultaneously.

market_context.valuation_assessment: The OECD PPP fair value for CAD/JPY is approximately 75–83 (derived from USD-based PPP: CAD/JPY PPP ~83 per CAD dossier). At 114.57, CAD is roughly 34–38% overvalued vs JPY on a PPP basis, making this one of the most stretched G10 cross-rates. The JPY's BIS REER sits in the bottom decile of post-1990 history while CAD's is modestly cheap at the 25th percentile — but the bilateral PPP gap overwhelmingly favours JPY appreciation over the strategic horizon.

positioning.net_pair_implication: Both positions are directionally aligned for CAD/JPY downside in one scenario: covering CAD shorts would strengthen CAD (pair-bullish), but unwinding JPY longs would weaken JPY (also pair-bullish) — creating a partially offsetting positioning picture. Net pair-implied positioning is roughly balanced-to-mildly-bearish, as the JPY long is the more unusual and larger-magnitude signal.
positioning.crowding_assessment: Balanced-to-mildly bearish at pair level; JPY long is the dominant and more unusual signal

pair_technicals.key_moving_averages: 50d SMA: 115.50 (spot below — near-term bearish); 100d SMA: 115.03 (spot below); 200d SMA: 113.37 (spot above — broad uptrend intact). MA configuration is mixed: spot has recently pierced below the 50-day but remains above the 200-day, indicating a corrective pullback within a broader uptrend rather than a trend reversal.
pair_technicals.chart_pattern: Rising wedge/triangle structure with price compressing between rising support and descending resistance, signalling weakening bullish momentum. April 30 bearish engulfing weekly candle (117.49 to 113.94 intraday) creates structural resistance zone at 117.00–117.50. Breakout below 115.00 favours bearish scenario with decline targets at 114.15, 113.00, and 112.15.

pair_volatility.rv_vs_iv: Variance risk premium is negligible or slightly negative (RV ≥ IV), suggesting directional spot positions are rewarded over options. Realised vol elevated at approximately 10–12% driven by the April 30 intervention-related spike.
pair_volatility.risk_reversals: Risk reversal environment post-BOJ intervention likely skews toward JPY-call premium (CAD/JPY downside protection is bid), consistent with the broader USD/JPY risk reversal shift post-intervention.
pair_volatility.vol_term_structure: Modest backwardation into the June 17 BOJ MPM; event premium priced for the June BOJ/FOMC cluster.

market_context.recent_regime: The past 6 weeks have been dominated by two phases: a sharp rally from ~103 to 117.49 driven by oil price strength (WTI $100+) and carry trade accumulation from February through late April, followed by a violent rejection from the highs on April 30 when BOJ intervention triggered a 350+ pip intraday collapse. Since May 1, the pair has stabilised in a 114–116 corridor with selling pressure on rallies toward 116 and buying interest near 114. The dominant regime is post-intervention range-trading with bearish skew from the BOJ hawkish hold and JPY-strengthening catalysts.

bilateral_carry_analysis.breakeven_move: Spot can move approximately 130 pips against (from 114.57 to ~113.27) before carry income is erased over 3M
bilateral_carry_analysis.carry_assessment: The BoC at 2.25% vs BOJ at 0.75% generates a +150bps policy rate differential in favour of long CAD/JPY positions. At the 2Y point, the differential is wider at approximately +180bps (Canada 2Y 2.85% vs Japan 2Y ~1.05%). Annualised carry for the pair is approximately +150bps, translating to roughly 0.47 JPY per day carry income per CAD unit (or ~14 pips per month at spot 114.57). The carry-to-vol ratio at +150bps / ~9.5% IV = 0.158, which is well below the 0.25 threshold typically considered attractive for carry trades — carry is not working effectively given current volatility. The trajectory of the carry differential is narrowing: BOJ is expected to hike to 1.00–1.25% over 1–6 months while BoC holds at 2.25%, compressing the gap from 150bps to approximately 100–125bps by Q4 2026, further undermining the carry case for long CAD/JPY.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The BoC-BOJ policy rate differential stands at +150bps (2.25% vs 0.75%), but the direction of travel is unambiguously toward compression. The BOJ's April 28 meeting delivered a 6-3 hawkish hold — the most fractured vote since 2016 — with three dissenters (Nakagawa, Takata, Tamura) favouring an immediate hike to 1.00%. The BoC held at 2.25% on April 29 with a hawkish hold framing, but the BoC's hawkishness supports CAD vs USD, not necessarily vs JPY where the BOJ is the more dynamic mover. At the 10Y point, the Canada-Japan spread is approximately 110bps (3.60% vs 2.50%), compressed from 200bps+ in 2024. OIS markets price ~35–40% probability of a BOJ June 17 hike while pricing zero BoC moves — meaning the bilateral rate path is asymmetrically bearish for CAD/JPY. The carry income of +150bps annualised sounds attractive, but the carry-to-vol ratio of 0.158 is well below the 0.25 threshold, and the breakeven move of ~130 pips over 3M is easily eclipsed by a single BOJ surprise.
key_bilateral_metrics: BoC rate: 2.25% vs BOJ rate: 0.75% = +150bps differential
Canada 2Y: 2.85% vs Japan 2Y: ~1.05% = +180bps
Canada 10Y: 3.60% vs Japan 10Y: 2.50% = +110bps (compressed from 200bps+ in 2024)
Carry-to-vol: 0.158 (unattractive; below 0.25 threshold)
BOJ June 17 hike probability: ~35-40% (BoC June 10: 0% probability of move)
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price a slow BOJ normalisation path; a June hike would shock CAD/JPY 150–250 pips lower, while a hold is the consensus base case. The asymmetry favours being positioned for pair downside.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Over 1–6 months, the bilateral rate differential is projected to compress from +150bps to +100–125bps as BOJ hikes to 1.00–1.25% (MUFG/Sony Financial projections) while BoC stays at 2.25%. The Fed is expected to cut 25–50bps, which benefits CAD vs USD but is irrelevant for the direct bilateral — what matters is the BoC-BOJ trajectory, which is firmly converging. The 10Y spread is projected to narrow from 110bps toward 75–85bps as JGB yields continue their structural ascent. The strategic carry compression undermines the primary pillar supporting long CAD/JPY positions. Each 25bp BOJ hike mechanically compresses the differential and shifts the carry-to-vol ratio deeper into unattractive territory, reducing the incentive for carry trade accumulation.
key_bilateral_metrics: Projected end-2026 BoC rate: 2.25–2.50% vs BOJ: 1.00–1.25%
Projected bilateral policy gap: 100–125bps (from 150bps now)
Projected 10Y Canada-Japan spread: 75–85bps (from 110bps)
MUFG BOJ path: 1.00% by Q2/Q3, 1.25% by Q4 2026
BoC path: hold at 2.25% through 2026, possible hike Q1 2027
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price slow convergence via one BOJ hike; we expect two, creating 25–50bps of additional mispricing that translates to 200–400 pips of CAD/JPY downside beyond forwards-implied.

factors.horizon_split: Both horizons bearish CAD/JPY, with strategic confidence (70) exceeding tactical (65) because the multi-meeting BOJ normalisation path provides more events for hawkish surprises to materialise. Tactical edge is the asymmetric June 17 BOJ outcome.

carry_analysis.summary: The +150bps BoC-BOJ differential generates carry income of ~14 pips/month but the carry-to-vol ratio of 0.158 is well below the 0.25 attractiveness threshold. The breakeven move of ~130 pips over 3M is easily eclipsed by a single BOJ meeting surprise. The trajectory is toward further compression (100–125bps by Q4 2026), making carry a declining rather than growing support for the pair.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: Canadian headline CPI at 2.4% YoY (March) compares to Japan core CPI at 1.8% and core-core at 2.4% — both economies face energy-driven inflation but through opposite channels. Canada benefits from oil-export revenue while Japan suffers from oil-import costs. The growth differential is narrowly in Canada's disfavour: Q4 2025 Canadian GDP contracted -0.6% annualised while Japan's was revised to +0.3% QoQ. Canadian unemployment at 6.7% is the highest in G10 vs Japan's 2.7% — the gap reflects divergent structural labour market conditions. The Shunto 2026 wage increase of 5.09% in Japan anchors BOJ normalisation, while Canadian wage growth at 4.7% is hot but offset by the 60% mortgage renewal headwind. On balance, the inflation picture supports BOJ hawkishness more directly than BoC hawkishness because BOJ explicitly revised its FY2026 core CPI forecast to 2.8%.
key_bilateral_metrics: Canada CPI: 2.4% vs Japan core CPI: 1.8% (but BOJ forecast: 2.8% FY2026)
Canada Q4 GDP: -0.6% ann. vs Japan Q4 GDP: +0.3% QoQ
Canada unemployment: 6.7% vs Japan: 2.7%
Shunto 2026 wage hike: 5.09% vs Canada wage growth: 4.7%
Next inflation data: May 19 Canada CPI, May 23 Tokyo CPI
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets expect Canadian CPI to drift higher on base effects while Japan's stays near 1.8%. An upside surprise in Tokyo CPI (2.0%) on May 23 would be the more impactful bilateral catalyst, boosting BOJ June hike odds and pressuring CAD/JPY lower.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, Canada faces stagflationary headwinds (weak growth, energy-driven CPI, mortgage stress) while Japan faces a similar but milder stagflation (0.5% FY2026 GDP, 2.8% CPI forecast). The critical bilateral difference is the policy response: BOJ has committed to normalisation on inflation persistence, while BoC is constrained by the USMCA risk and weak growth. The growth differential does not clearly favour either currency — both economies are underperforming — but the inflation differential supports a more aggressive BOJ than BoC, which is bearish for the pair.
key_bilateral_metrics: BoC 2026 GDP: 1.2% vs BOJ FY2026 GDP: 0.5%
Canada's growth headwind: mortgage renewal stress, 60% cohort
Japan's growth headwind: energy-import costs, weak fiscal multiplier
BOJ FY2026 CPI: 2.8% → supports 2+ hikes; BoC: hold
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price modest growth for both economies. The bilateral growth differential is near-zero, but the inflation-policy response divergence is the tradable signal.

factors.horizon_split: Both horizons mildly bearish CAD/JPY with same confidence (55). The inflation-growth mix doesn't strongly differentiate between horizons.

factors.weight_adjustment_reason: CAD/JPY is a high-beta risk proxy pair; risk factor deserves higher weight at both horizons

tactical.bilateral_analysis: CAD/JPY is a classic risk-on pair — long CAD (commodity/risk proxy) vs short JPY (safe-haven/funding currency). With VIX at ~17, equities near records, and oil above $100, the current risk environment superficially favours pair upside. However, JPY's safe-haven correlation has been compromised in 2026 by the energy shock (yen weakened during risk-off episodes because oil imports dominated the safe-haven bid). This creates an unusual dynamic for CAD/JPY: in risk-on, both CAD and JPY weaken (oil up helps CAD, hurts JPY) — amplifying the pair upside. In risk-off, both currencies should strengthen but the CAD-oil correlation dominates — creating compressed pair moves. The Nikkei VI at ~29.5 vs VIX at 17 signals Japan-specific stress that dilutes the risk-on signal. SPX-Nikkei 30-day correlation at 0.15 is unusually low, suggesting Japan idiosyncratic factors dominate. Net: the risk-on environment supports carry trades, but BOJ intervention creates an asymmetric cap that neutralises the signal.
key_bilateral_metrics: VIX: 17.4 (constructive)
Nikkei VI: ~29.5 (elevated Japan-specific stress)
SPX-Nikkei correlation: 0.15 (decoupled)
CAD/JPY risk-beta: high (risk-on pair), but intervention-capped
AUD/JPY at ~111 (risk barometer for JPY crosses: off highs)
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price benign risk-on continuation. The pair's risk-on sensitivity is real but capped by the 117.49 intervention ceiling, creating a truncated distribution.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, if Iran ceasefire firms and oil normalises toward $80–90, the risk-on carry trade tailwind fades. Japan's safe-haven character should reassert as the energy-import overlay dissipates. A US recession scenario (CNBC flagged 'sleepwalking into recession') would trigger classical JPY rally while compressing CAD via export channel — a double-negative for CAD/JPY. The August 2024 carry unwind episode (USD/JPY dropped 15 figures in weeks) is the reference for tail risk in this pair; conditions for a partial repeat are building (compressed differentials, elevated JPY vol).
key_bilateral_metrics: Japan NIIP: +$3.3T (structural repatriation reflex in risk-off)
Canada export dependency on US: 75% (recession transmission channel)
CAD/JPY Aug 2024 unwind: ~12 figures in 2 weeks
Oil normalisation scenario (Brent $80): bearish CAD/JPY via double compression
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price continued risk-on; we see meaningful tail risk of a risk-off event that would hit CAD/JPY from both legs simultaneously.

factors.horizon_split: Tactical Neutral reflects the current benign risk backdrop; strategic Bearish reflects the probability of at least one risk-off episode over 1–6 months that would disproportionately hit this high-beta pair.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The combined positioning picture is unusual. CAD leg: leveraged funds net short -54,703 contracts (70th percentile of net short range) — if squeezed, CAD strengthens (pair-bullish). JPY leg: leveraged funds net long +56,400 contracts (75th percentile of net long range) — if unwound, JPY weakens (pair-bullish). Both positions, if reversed, would push CAD/JPY higher — but the probability of simultaneous reversal is low. The more likely scenario is selective: BOJ hawkish surprise triggers JPY strength (lev fund longs confirmed) while CAD shorts persist on USMCA uncertainty — net pair-bearish. The contrarian risk is a CAD short squeeze coinciding with JPY long unwind (both triggered by oil spike / risk-on explosion) — pair-bullish but lower probability (~20%).
key_bilateral_metrics: CAD lev fund net: -54,703 (short, squeeze-prone)
JPY lev fund net: +56,400 (long, unusual configuration)
Net pair implication: offsetting; depends on which leg triggers
Open interest: CAD 277,790; JPY 372,780 — healthy engagement both legs
Risk reversal: JPY-call premium (pair-downside protection bid)
tactical.what_is_priced: Positioning is balanced at the pair level — neither extreme long nor extreme short. The directional signal is muted.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Over 1–6 months, the JPY long position is the key variable. If BOJ delivers 1–2 hikes as expected, the JPY longs are validated and may extend — this is pair-bearish. If BOJ disappoints, JPY longs unwind rapidly (5–8 figures USD/JPY upside, translating to 4–6 figures CAD/JPY upside). The CAD short unwind post-USMCA (if 75% annual-reviews outcome) provides a mechanical CAD bid worth ~$8B in CFTC terms — this would push CAD/JPY higher but the magnitude depends on whether JPY longs persist simultaneously. Expected-value weighted: 60% JPY longs confirmed (pair-bearish) × 4–6 figure move + 25% JPY longs unwind (pair-bullish) × 3–5 figures + 15% both trigger simultaneously (neutral) = net mildly bearish.
key_bilateral_metrics: JPY lev fund long at 56K: historically unusual, confirmation-dependent
CAD short unwind expected: ~$8B notional post-USMCA
Combined pair positioning: net neutral at aggregate level
Historic analogue: Aug 2024 carry unwind saw CAD/JPY drop ~12 figures
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price continued range-trading; the bilateral positioning signal is genuinely neutral.

factors.horizon_split: Both horizons Neutral. Positioning does not drive the pair directionally at either horizon.

tactical.combined_positioning_signal: At the pair level, CAD shorts (-54,703) and JPY longs (+56,400) are each squeeze-prone in isolation but offset at the cross level — net pair positioning is balanced-to-mildly-bearish.
tactical.unwind_trigger: Simultaneous CAD short squeeze and JPY long liquidation requires oil spike above $120 + BOJ dovish hold + USMCA-positive headline coinciding. Probability ~15%.
tactical.historical_analogue: August 2024 carry unwind: CAD/JPY dropped ~12 figures in 2 weeks as JPY longs were built and CAD carry positions were exited simultaneously.
tactical.contrarian_case: The combined positioning at the pair level does not present an extreme contrarian signal. The risk of a simultaneous squeeze on both legs (CAD shorts covered + JPY longs liquidated) exists but requires a specific catalyst: oil spike above $120 + BOJ dovish hold + USMCA-positive headline. Probability: ~15%. This scenario would push CAD/JPY rapidly toward 117–119 against the consensus bearish view.

strategic.combined_positioning_signal: The cleared JPY shorts remove the historic squeeze fuel that made every CAD/JPY rally violent. Future rallies will be slower and more fundamentally driven. The JPY long position creates a new contrarian risk if BOJ disappoints — but requires sustained dovish BOJ communication.
strategic.unwind_trigger: BOJ dovish hold through September 2026 with explicit growth-caution language would unwind JPY longs, pushing USD/JPY higher and dragging CAD/JPY up 4–6 figures from current levels.
strategic.historical_analogue: Pre-2024 JPY short squeeze episodes: when USD/JPY reversed from 160, CAD/JPY fell 8–10 figures concurrently as carry trades were unwound across all JPY crosses.
strategic.contrarian_case: The JPY long position creates a new contrarian risk if BOJ disappoints — this requires sustained dovish BOJ communication that contradicts the April 6-3 vote. Strategic contrarian upside: 118–120 if BOJ credibly defers all 2026 hikes and Iranian oil risk re-emerges. The contrarian case is low probability (~20%) but would generate a rapid 350–550 pip move against the consensus bearish view.

factors.weight_adjustment_reason: Oil's double amplification (exporter vs importer) is uniquely important for this pair; tactical weight increased from 0.06 to 0.09

tactical.bilateral_analysis: This is where the bilateral interaction creates a signal that doesn't exist in either standalone view. Oil above $100 is simultaneously bullish CAD (exporter) and bearish JPY (importer with 95.9% ME crude dependence). The net commodity exposure for long CAD/JPY is powerfully positive: oil up → ++CAD (exporter), −−JPY (importer). This is the single strongest bilateral tailwind for the pair, explaining the rally from 101 to 117 over 12 months. Canada's Q4 2025 current account narrowed to -C$0.7B while Japan's February current account surplus was ¥3,932.7B — but the goods-balance compression (Japan imports +10.9% YoY) reflects the energy-cost damage. Tactically, oil at $102–106 sustains the bilateral terms-of-trade advantage for long CAD/JPY.
key_bilateral_metrics: Canada: oil exporter, WCS price ~$86–90/bbl (highly profitable)
Japan: 95.9% ME oil dependence, trade balance miss in March
Net oil exposure for CAD/JPY: powerfully bullish (double amplification)
Each $10/bbl WTI move: ~+200–300 pip CAD/JPY impact (estimated bilateral sensitivity)
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price oil normalisation to $90–95 by late 2026. If oil holds above $100, the terms-of-trade support for long CAD/JPY is underpriced.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, the oil tailwind for CAD/JPY faces a binary path. If Iran ceasefire firms and Brent reverts to $80, the bilateral terms-of-trade advantage collapses — Japan's external position improves (primary income surplus dominates, energy-import drag fades) while Canada loses the oil-revenue windfall. This scenario is bearish for the pair on a commodity basis. If oil sustains above $100, the current bilateral advantage persists. The forward curve prices Brent at $85–90 by Q4 2026 — this baseline implies the terms-of-trade tailwind for CAD/JPY fading over the strategic horizon. Japan has nearly $40 billion in bilateral trade with Canada, with Canada's merchandise exports to Japan totaling $14.5 billion in 2025 while imports from Japan were $21.1 billion. 70% of the cars manufactured in Canada are made by Japanese companies, creating structural two-way investment flows. The March 2026 Comprehensive Strategic Partnership signed by Carney and Takaichi covers defence, energy, critical minerals, and trade ties — a structural positive for bilateral flows but not a near-term FX catalyst.
key_bilateral_metrics: Brent forward curve Q4 2026: ~$85–90 (implies fading oil tailwind)
Japan NIIP: +$3.3T (structural income cushion as oil fades)
Bilateral trade: ~$40B (Canada exports: $14.5B; imports: $21.1B)
Carney-Takaichi Strategic Partnership: energy/minerals/defence (March 2026)
Net commodity scenario: oil $80 → bearish CAD/JPY; oil $100+ → bullish
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price oil normalisation; we agree that the bilateral terms-of-trade advantage for long CAD/JPY fades over 1–6 months. Net Neutral.

factors.horizon_split: Tactical Bullish (oil above $100 supporting the pair now) vs Strategic Neutral (forward curve prices oil normalisation, eroding the bilateral edge). Inflection point: Iran ceasefire firming and Brent breaking $90.

net_commodity_exposure.base_commodity_profile: Canada is a major oil exporter (WCS ~$86–90/bbl); oil revenues directly support CAD current account and fiscal position. Higher oil prices are unambiguously CAD-positive.
net_commodity_exposure.quote_commodity_profile: Japan imports 95.9% of its crude oil from the Middle East; higher oil prices directly worsen Japan's trade balance and current account, weakening JPY through the terms-of-trade channel.
net_commodity_exposure.net_effect_calculation: Step 1: Oil rises → CAD strengthens (exporter benefit: improved fiscal revenues, current account, commodity-linked capital flows). Step 2: Oil rises → JPY weakens (importer cost: worsened trade balance, higher import bill, current account deterioration, BoJ constrained from raising rates to offset oil-driven inflation). Step 3: Net effect on CAD/JPY = CAD up + JPY down = double amplification — the pair rises MORE than either individual currency move against USD. Each $10/bbl WTI move translates to approximately +200–300 pips of CAD/JPY upside. Step 4: The reverse holds: oil falls → CAD down + JPY up = double compression, accelerating pair downside beyond what either leg alone would imply.
net_commodity_exposure.scenario_oil_up_10pct: WTI $110–117: CAD strengthens ~1.5–2% vs USD; JPY weakens ~1–1.5% vs USD on terms-of-trade deterioration; net CAD/JPY upside of approximately +3–3.5% (~340–400 pips) — double the individual currency moves.
net_commodity_exposure.scenario_oil_down_10pct: WTI $92–95: CAD weakens ~1.5–2% vs USD; JPY strengthens ~1–1.5% vs USD on improved import bill; net CAD/JPY downside of approximately -3–3.5% (~340–400 pips) — double compression from both legs.
net_commodity_exposure.dominant_commodity_driver: Crude oil (WTI/Brent) is the overwhelmingly dominant commodity driver for CAD/JPY — it is the only major commodity that simultaneously affects both legs in opposite directions, creating the unique double-amplification effect that makes this pair unusually sensitive to oil price changes relative to any other G10 cross.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The guidance divergence is the key bilateral signal. BOJ delivered the most hawkish vote since 2016 (6-3, three dissenters at 1.00%) with Ueda explicitly stating 'real interest rates are at significantly low levels' — textbook hawkish language preparing for imminent normalisation. The BOJ Outlook Report raised FY2026 core CPI to 2.8% — the largest single-meeting upward revision in its history. In contrast, BoC's April 29 hawkish hold was directed at USD/CAD (supporting CAD vs USD), not at the JPY cross. Macklem's 'stand ready to respond' language is conditional on USMCA and inflation expectations — not a commitment to hike. The BOJ is guiding toward an imminent policy change; the BoC is guiding toward extended hold. This guidance divergence is unambiguously bearish for CAD/JPY.
key_bilateral_metrics: BOJ vote: 6-3 hawkish hold (3 dissenters at 1.00%)
BoC: unanimous hold at 2.25% with conditional hawkish bias
BOJ FY2026 CPI revision: +0.9pp to 2.8% (record upward revision)
Ueda: 'real interest rates significantly low'
Macklem: 'little evidence oil has fed through' + 'stand ready'
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price BOJ guidance as credible but slow-acting; we see the June 17 meeting as a 35–45% live hike probability — higher than market pricing.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, the BOJ guidance framework has decisively shifted to normalisation bias. With three voters already at 1.00% and Ueda signalling real rates are too low, the multi-meeting path toward 1.00–1.25% is the central case. BoC's strategic posture is hawkish hold transitioning to possible hike in Q1 2027 — a slower trajectory. The July 15 BoC MPR (post-USMCA) is the key BoC event, but it's unlikely to deliver a rate change. The BOJ has the October Outlook Report to further entrench the normalisation narrative. The credibility differential favours BOJ: markets increasingly trust Ueda's transparent communication style, while BoC credibility is high but directionally static.
key_bilateral_metrics: BOJ next MPM: June 16–17 (hike probability 35–40%)
BoC next decision: June 10 (100% hold)
BOJ strategic path: 1.00–1.25% by year-end
BoC strategic path: 2.25% through 2026, possible 2.50% in 2027
Guidance divergence: BOJ actively normalising, BoC on hold
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price one BOJ hike (July) and BoC hold. We see two BOJ hikes as more likely, creating additional compression of ~25bps beyond market pricing.

factors.horizon_split: Both bearish CAD/JPY; strategic confidence (65) exceeds tactical (60) because the multi-meeting BOJ path provides more opportunities for hawkish delivery.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The pair faces event risk from both legs: USMCA review deadline (July 1) is CAD-specific, while the Iran ceasefire trajectory is JPY-specific via oil. In the tactical window, the Iran ceasefire status is the dominant binary for CAD/JPY — a ceasefire firming would drop oil, hurting CAD and helping JPY (pair-bearish), while a ceasefire failure would spike oil, helping CAD and hurting JPY (pair-bullish). The USMCA risk is CAD-negative if escalation occurs, but is less directly impactful on CAD/JPY than on USD/CAD because the JPY leg is independently driven. MOF intervention risk is live at the 160 USD/JPY level — at current CAD/JPY levels (~115), a USD/JPY move to 160 would push CAD/JPY toward 117–118 (near the 52-week high), likely triggering intervention concern.
key_bilateral_metrics: Iran ceasefire: fragile; binary oil impact on the pair
USMCA deadline: July 1 (~8 weeks out; 15% withdrawal probability)
MOF intervention: demonstrated at 160 USD/JPY; CAD/JPY cap ~117–118
BOJ June 17 MPM: primary pair event in tactical window
Fed Chair transition May 15: secondary USD impact
tactical.what_is_priced: Markets price modest risk premium. Event risk is balanced between bullish (USMCA-positive + oil sustains) and bearish (ceasefire firms + BOJ hike) scenarios.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, the event risk balance tilts bearish for CAD/JPY. The BOJ has 2–3 MPMs in the window (June, July, September), each a live hike risk. Iran ceasefire is more likely to firm than collapse over 6 months (base case), which removes the oil tailwind for the pair. USMCA resolution (75% annual reviews per Jefferies) would remove CAD uncertainty but also remove the trade-policy risk premium that has kept CAD depressed — paradoxically, a clean resolution is marginally CAD-positive but not enough to offset BOJ normalisation.
key_bilateral_metrics: BOJ MPMs in strategic window: June 17, July 31, September 19
USMCA resolution: July 1 (75% annual reviews)
Iran ceasefire 6M base case: firms with Brent $80–90
Strategic event bias: BOJ hikes (pair-bearish) + oil normalisation (pair-bearish)
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price one BOJ hike and oil normalisation — broadly consistent with our view. No strong mispricing.

factors.horizon_split: Tactical Neutral (balanced near-term risks) vs Strategic Bearish (BOJ normalisation + oil fade = structural pair-bearish over 1–6M).

tactical.bilateral_analysis: Canadian sovereign ratings (AAA S&P/Moody's/DBRS) materially exceed Japan's (A+/A1/A). However, this gap has been stable for decades and does not generate FX flows at current levels. Japan's 250% gross debt/GDP vs Canada's ~50% federal is the elephant in the room, but BOJ ownership of ~50% of JGBs suppresses refinancing risk. The Takaichi fiscal stimulus (¥21.3T supplementary + ¥120T budget) creates ongoing JGB supply pressure that has pushed 30Y/40Y yields to record areas — but this is already priced in the JGB curve. No tactical fiscal catalyst for either side.
key_bilateral_metrics: Canada rating: AAA; Japan rating: A+/A1/A (6-notch gap)
Canada federal debt/GDP: ~50%; Japan gross debt/GDP: ~250%
JGB long-end: 30Y ~3.85%, 40Y 4.21% (record-area)
Fiscal policy bias: Carney expansionary ($37.5B/5Y); Takaichi expansionary (¥21.3T)
tactical.what_is_priced: The rating gap is fully priced and structurally persistent. Neutral on both horizons.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Same as tactical. The fiscal differential does not drive the pair at either horizon. The only scenario where it becomes material is if BOJ normalisation pushes JGB 10Y through 3%, raising fiscal sustainability concerns that constrain further BOJ hiking — this would be pair-bullish as it removes the rate-convergence catalyst. Low probability (~15%) within the strategic window.
key_bilateral_metrics: JGB 10Y threshold for fiscal concern: ~3.0%
Each 100bp JGB yield rise: ~2% GDP additional debt servicing
Fiscal constraint on BOJ: theoretical but not yet binding

factors.horizon_split: Both Neutral at similar confidence. Fiscal does not drive the pair.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The price data shows spot at 114.57 below the 50-day SMA (115.50) but above the 100-day SMA (115.03) and 200-day SMA (113.37). The MA alignment is modestly bearish at the front end (spot < 50d) but the broader uptrend from the 101.25 low remains intact (spot > 200d). The recent rejection from 117.49 on April 30 with a massive intraday range (117.49 to 113.94) creates a bearish engulfing pattern on weekly timeframes. TradingView analysis identifies CAD/JPY trading inside a rising wedge/triangle with key bearish breakdown targets at 116.00 and 115.00. Pivot support at 115.65, first support at 114.70, and first resistance at 116.54 are the near-term technical levels from community analysis. The 200-day SMA at 113.37 is the critical strategic support — a break below would flip the long-term trend regime from bullish to neutral. Investing.com's daily technical signal reads 'Strong Buy' based on moving averages with 12 buy signals and 0 sell signals, creating a contrarian setup if the fundamental picture deteriorates.
key_bilateral_metrics: Spot: 114.57; 50d SMA: 115.50 (spot below = near-term bearish)
100d SMA: 115.03; 200d SMA: 113.37 (spot above = broad uptrend intact)
Key support: 114.00 (Fib 61.8%), 113.37 (200d SMA), 112.15 (chart target)
Key resistance: 115.65 (pivot), 116.54 (first resistance), 117.49 (52-wk high)
April 30 bearish engulfing: 117.49 → 113.94 intraday
tactical.what_is_priced: The vol market prices moderate event premium for June BOJ/FOMC cluster. The bearish engulfing from the April 30 intervention creates a structural resistance zone at 117.00–117.50 that caps pair upside.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Aggregated bank forecasts project CAD/JPY at 112.18 by mid-2026 and 111.06 by year-end 2026, declining further to 111.00 by mid-2027. This consensus view implies ~300 pips of downside from current levels over the strategic horizon. The pair's broader structural picture shows the rally from 101 to 117 as a commodity-cycle-driven move that is now entering the reversal phase as oil normalises and BOJ normalises rates. The 200d SMA at 113.37 is the strategic pivot — a sustained break below would trigger CTA trend-following flows to the downside and accelerate toward the 110–111 consensus target.
key_bilateral_metrics: Consensus forecast: 112.18 mid-2026, 111.06 year-end 2026
200d SMA: 113.37 (strategic pivot)
Below 200d: CTA flows flip to short, targeting 110–111
Strategic range expectation: 108–117
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price gradual pair decline toward 111 — we are aligned with consensus directionally but see the path as potentially faster if BOJ delivers June hike.

factors.horizon_split: Both bearish; strategic consensus alignment provides confidence in directional view.

expression_assessment.vol_landscape_summary: CAD/JPY 1M implied volatility estimated at 9–10% (post-intervention, slightly elevated). Realised vol over 20 days at ~10–12% driven by the April 30 intervention spike. The variance risk premium is negligible or slightly negative (RV ≥ IV), suggesting directional spot positions are more efficiently rewarded than options-based expressions. Vol term structure shows modest backwardation into the June 17 BOJ MPM.
expression_assessment.directional_expression: Spot short CAD/JPY is the most efficient vehicle for the bearish view at both horizons. The carry cost of -150bps annualised (~14 pips/month) is manageable against a 300+ pip directional target. Options are less attractive because IV is mid-range and risk reversals likely trade at a premium for JPY protection (pair-downside), making bought puts expensive relative to the spot carry cost. Strategic expression: short 3–6M CAD/JPY forward capturing both directional decline and forward roll-down (~250 pips of forward roll into the 6M contract).
expression_assessment.event_timing_consideration: June 17 BOJ MPM is the primary event that affects expression choice. A short position through this event captures the asymmetric hike risk (150–250 pip move lower on hike). Entering before the May 23 Tokyo CPI allows capture of the pre-event drift. Position sizing should be reduced ahead of June 10 BoC and June 17 FOMC/BOJ simultaneous events to manage binary risk.
expression_assessment.liquidity_note: CAD/JPY is a reasonably liquid G10 cross but less liquid than USD/CAD or USD/JPY. Typical bid-ask spread is 1–3 pips in normal conditions; widens to 5–10 pips around major events. The intervention-defined ceiling at ~117.50 provides a natural stop-loss reference for short positions. Execution should be sized to avoid market impact, particularly given the 372,780 JPY open interest and 277,790 CAD open interest suggesting adequate institutional depth.

factors.weight_adjustment_reason: Non-USD cross; USD funding less directly relevant to CAD/JPY dynamics

tactical.bilateral_analysis: As a non-USD cross, CAD/JPY's sensitivity to USD funding conditions operates through the vehicle-currency channel — both legs are typically traded against USD before being crossed. USD funding conditions are stable with SOFR near 3.50–3.60%. Cross-currency basis for both CAD/USD and JPY/USD is not directly available from the dossiers for May 2026. The JPY/USD basis is structurally negative (Japanese institutions pay premium for USD), while CAD/USD basis is typically tight. The dollar smile places USD in the neutral middle — neither regime extreme that would systematically distort CAD/JPY via the vehicle currency channel. The Fed's dovish bias weakens USD against both CAD and JPY, with the net effect on the cross approximately neutral (both legs move similarly vs USD in this scenario).
key_bilateral_metrics: Fed Funds: 3.50–3.75% (dovish bias, expected 25–50bp cuts 2026)
DXY: 98.35 (neutral on smile framework)
JPY/USD basis: structurally negative (~-30 to -50bp)
CAD/USD basis: typically tight
Vehicle currency impact on cross: modest, ~neutral
tactical.what_is_priced: USD funding neutrality is priced; no material edge for CAD/JPY via this channel.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, Fed cuts weaken USD against both CAD and JPY — but the BoC-BOJ divergence means JPY benefits more (BOJ hiking while BoC holds). The structural funding-currency role of JPY weakens with each BOJ hike, reducing the incentive for institutional JPY-funded carry trades including CAD/JPY. As the carry differential compresses from 150bps to 100–125bps and carry-to-vol deteriorates, the pair loses its carry trade attraction. The Japanese institutional channel (lifers, banks reducing USD-funded positions as hedging costs improve) creates a secondary JPY-supportive flow. Net: mildly bearish via carry-trade decommissioning.
key_bilateral_metrics: BoC end-2026: 2.25% (hold)
Carry trade decommissioning: structural headwind for long CAD/JPY
Japanese lifer repatriation: activated below USD/JPY 150 → cascades to crosses
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price gradual carry fade; we agree. Mildly bearish signal.

factors.horizon_split: Tactical Neutral (no near-term Fed catalyst); Strategic Bearish (cumulative carry compression and funding-currency role weakening).

adjustments.reason: CAD/JPY is a high-beta risk proxy pair; risk factor deserves higher weight at both tactical and strategic horizons
Oil's double amplification (exporter vs importer) is uniquely important for this pair at the tactical horizon; tactical weight increased from 0.06 to 0.09
Non-USD cross; USD funding less directly relevant to CAD/JPY bilateral dynamics

tactical.score_interpretation: Net score -0.271 maps to Bearish CAD/JPY with moderate conviction 2/5. The offsetting oil tailwind (Factor 5 at +0.054) and binary BOJ June outcome uncertainty cap conviction. The dominant bearish drivers are Policy & Rates (-0.156) and Inflation & Growth (-0.099), reflecting the BOJ hawkish pivot and rate convergence dynamic.
tactical.rationale: The tactical bearish CAD/JPY view rests primarily on the asymmetric BOJ June 17 outcome and intervention-capped upside. Factor 1 (Policy & Rates) contributes the largest weighted drag at -0.156, reflecting the +150bps carry differential being on a compression trajectory — the BOJ's 6-3 April vote with three dissenters at 1.00% makes the June meeting a live 35–45% hike probability that markets are underpricing. Factor 2 (Inflation & Growth) adds -0.099 as the BOJ's FY2026 core CPI revision to 2.8% embeds structural normalisation pressure that exceeds the BoC's hawkish-hold framing. The single offsetting bullish factor is External Balance (+0.054), where oil above $100 creates a powerful double-amplification tailwind — Canada exports it, Japan imports it — that directly supports pair upside. However, this tailwind is priced and faces binary Iran ceasefire risk. Positioning (Factor 4) is genuinely neutral at the pair level — CAD shorts and JPY longs are each squeeze-prone in isolation but offset at the cross level. The intervention-defined ceiling at ~117.50 creates a truncated upside distribution that structurally favours short positions, as the maximum pair upside (~300 pips to 52-week high) is materially less than the potential downside (~500+ pips to 109–110 area if BOJ surprises). Conviction is capped at 2/5 because the 4-week window lacks a high-probability catalyst — the BOJ Summary of Opinions (~May 7) and Tokyo CPI (May 23) are the main events, neither of which is likely to definitively resolve the June hike question. Factor 6 (Guidance & Credibility) adds -0.042, reflecting the stark contrast between Ueda's explicit 'real rates are significantly low' framing and Macklem's conditional hold language. Market structure (Factor 9, -0.028) confirms the bearish technical picture: spot below the 50d SMA, weekly bearish engulfing from the April 30 rejection, and the rising wedge breakdown signalling momentum exhaustion.
tactical.g10_relative_ranking: Among JPY crosses, CAD/JPY ranks as the 3rd most attractive short behind AUD/JPY (wider rate gap compression, higher risk-beta) and GBP/JPY (BoE cutting into BOJ hiking). CAD/JPY is preferred over NZD/JPY (matched 2.25% rate, no differential edge) and EUR/JPY (ECB potentially hiking on energy CPI, muddying the bilateral). Among CAD crosses, CAD/JPY downside is more attractive than USD/CAD downside because the BOJ is more actively normalising than the Fed.

conditions: BOJ Summary of Opinions shows 3 dissenters without escalation to 4+
Tokyo CPI (May 23) prints in-line with consensus at 1.5–1.7%
Oil remains in $100–108 range; Iran ceasefire status unchanged
base_case.path: Choppy drift lower; pair tests 200d SMA at 113.37 at least once, with rallies toward 115.50 (50d SMA) used as selling opportunities. Pattern shape: slow grind lower with 100-pip daily ranges and occasional spike-and-fade on oil/Iran headlines.

CAD leveraged fund shorts begin covering on USMCA-positive headline
BOJ Summary of Opinions shows 4+ members open to June hike
Tokyo CPI prints above 2.0%, sharply boosting June hike probability
Oil drops below $95 on ceasefire news

tactical.path_expectation: The 2–4 week path is most likely a choppy drift lower. Expect the pair to test the 200d SMA at 113.37 at least once, with rallies toward 115.50 (50d SMA) used as selling opportunities. The May 23 Tokyo CPI is the primary catalyst — an upside surprise accelerates the move toward 113. The BOJ Summary of Opinions (due ~May 7–9) may provide an early signal. Pattern shape: slow grind lower with 100-pip daily ranges and occasional spike-and-fade on oil/Iran headlines. Pullback levels for re-shorts: 115.50–116.00. The intervention ceiling at 117.50 provides a hard cap that limits upside, creating an asymmetric distribution where the downside path is longer than the upside path from current spot.

risk_reward.base_case_payoff: +43 pips net (57 pips directional gain minus 14 pips carry cost)
risk_reward.downside_case_loss: -257 pips total (243 pips adverse move plus 14 pips carry cost)
risk_reward.asymmetry_assessment: Modestly positive expected value but skewed by the 25% upside scenario; risk management via 117.00 stop is essential to preserve the positive expected value profile.

strategic.score_interpretation: Net score -0.413 maps to Bearish CAD/JPY with conviction 3/5. Seven factors contribute negatively, three are neutral, zero are bullish. The dominant contributors are Policy & Rates (-0.168), Guidance & Credibility (-0.078), and Inflation & Growth (-0.055). The key shift from tactical is External Balance moving from Bullish to Neutral as oil normalisation is priced over the strategic horizon.
strategic.rationale: The strategic bearish case is driven by the convergence of four reinforcing factors: BOJ rate normalisation compressing the carry differential from 150bps to 100–125bps (Factor 1, -0.168); BOJ guidance explicitly anchoring a multi-hike path via the 2.8% FY2026 CPI revision (Factor 6, -0.078); oil price normalisation toward $80–90 removing the bilateral terms-of-trade double-amplification (Factor 5 shifting to Neutral); and the risk-off tail risk that uniquely punishes this high-beta pair from both legs simultaneously (Factor 3, -0.040). The oil factor is the swing variable — tactically it's pair-bullish at $100+, but strategically the forward curve prices normalisation that eliminates the pair's primary tailwind. Both currencies are sensitive to oil: CAD as an exporter benefits from rising oil while Japan as an importer is hurt, making long CAD/JPY a 'risk-on' trade that becomes crowded and vulnerable to shocks. The BOJ normalisation path is the structural anchor: MUFG projects 1.00% by Q2/Q3 and 1.25% by Q4 2026, compared to BoC's static 2.25% — compressing the bilateral gap by 50–75bps over 6 months. Aggregated bank research projects CAD/JPY declining to 112.18 by mid-2026 and 111.06 by year-end 2026, fully consistent with our bearish view. The intervention ceiling at ~117.50 structurally limits pair upside while the BOJ normalisation path provides a steady bearish undercurrent. Conviction is 3/5 because the view aligns with consensus direction but the oil/Iran tail risk creates meaningful two-way variance. The edge is in timing — we expect faster decline on BOJ June hike — rather than direction. USD Liquidity (Factor 10, -0.030) contributes a secondary bearish input via carry-trade decommissioning as the JPY funding-currency role erodes with each BOJ hike, reducing institutional appetite for CAD/JPY long positions. Inflation & Growth (Factor 2, -0.055) reinforces the policy divergence: BOJ's 2.8% FY2026 CPI forecast and 5.09% Shunto wage growth provide durable normalisation support that BoC's constrained-by-USMCA hawkishness cannot match.
strategic.g10_relative_ranking: CAD/JPY ranks as the 3rd most attractive JPY cross short over the strategic horizon, behind AUD/JPY (wider carry compression + higher risk-beta) and EUR/JPY (ECB at 2% with uncertain path vs BOJ hiking). Preferred over NZD/JPY (no carry edge) and USD/JPY (more crowded trade). Among CAD shorts, CAD/JPY is preferred over CAD/CHF (SNB intervention risk) but less attractive than short USD/CAD (cleaner strategic CAD-positive expression with less BOJ binary risk).

base_case.pair_target: CAD/JPY 110–112 by Q4 2026 (~250–450 pips of downside from 114.57)
conditions: BOJ delivers one hike (June or July) to 1.00%
Iran ceasefire holds with Brent stabilising at $80–90
BoC holds at 2.25% through 2026; no major risk-off shock
base_case.path: Three-phase path: Phase 1 (May–June) consolidation in 113–116 with grinding lower bias; Phase 2 (July–September) directional decline to 110–113 as BOJ hikes and oil normalises; Phase 3 (October–November) final grind toward 108–111 as second BOJ hike or Fed cut materialises.

conditions: Iran ceasefire fails with Brent $130+; BOJ defers all hikes on growth concerns
Canadian data surprises positively; oil sustains above $110
BOJ delivers two hikes to 1.25%; Fed cuts 50bp+
Brent drops below $80; US recession concerns trigger full carry unwind

strategic.path_expectation: The strategic path unfolds in three phases. Phase 1 (May–June): consolidation in 113–116 range with grinding lower bias; BOJ June 17 MPM is the potential catalytic event that could accelerate Phase 2. Phase 2 (July–September): directional decline as BOJ delivers hike(s) and oil normalises; target 110–113 range; 200d SMA break (~113.37) triggers CTA selling and accelerates toward 110–111 consensus target. Phase 3 (October–November): final grind toward 108–111 as carry differential compression completes and second BOJ hike or Fed cut materialises. Volatility expectations: 9–11% IV through Phase 1; 8–10% in Phase 3 as event premium decays. Pullback levels for adding shorts: 115.50–116.00 in Phase 1; 113.00–114.00 in Phase 2. The intervention ceiling at 117.50 remains a hard structural cap on upside throughout all three phases.

risk_reward.downside_case_loss: -530 pips total (~4.6%) — 443 pips adverse move plus 87 pips carry cost

consensus_divergence.bank_consensus_forecast: Aggregated bank forecasts project CAD/JPY at 112.18 by mid-2026 and 111.06 by year-end 2026 (source: Exchange Rates UK aggregated bank research, Traders Union).
consensus_divergence.our_tactical_view: Bearish CAD/JPY; target 113–114 over 2–4 weeks with conviction 2/5
consensus_divergence.our_strategic_view: Bearish CAD/JPY; target 110–112 by Q3 2026 (potentially earlier than consensus year-end timing), conviction 3/5
consensus_divergence.divergence_direction: Directionally aligned with consensus but with more front-loaded path
consensus_divergence.divergence_magnitude: ~100–200 pips ahead of consensus timing; consensus sees 111 by year-end, we see 110–112 achievable by Q3
consensus_divergence.why_we_differ: We assign higher probability (~45%) to a BOJ June hike vs consensus (~35%) given the unprecedented 6-3 April vote. Our reading of Ueda's 'real rates significantly low' language as more imminent-action framing than consensus interprets it. Additionally, we weight the oil normalisation scenario as more likely over the 6-month window (forward curve pricing Brent $85–90 by Q4), which removes the bilateral terms-of-trade support faster than consensus models. The combination of earlier BOJ delivery and faster oil normalisation produces a steeper front-loaded decline path vs the consensus gradual drift.
consensus_divergence.payoff_if_right: +200 pips vs consensus timing (being short 3M earlier than consensus on BOJ June hike delivers early carry roll-down plus directional gain)
consensus_divergence.risk_if_wrong: If BOJ holds through September, the directional view still holds for October/November — we lose the timing edge but not the directional view; downside is giving back ~50 pips of forward roll advantage

cross_horizon_consistency.explanation: Tactical and strategic views are aligned (both Bearish CAD/JPY), with strategic conviction higher (3 vs 2) because the multi-meeting BOJ normalisation path provides more opportunities for the thesis to materialise. The tactical horizon lacks a high-probability near-term catalyst (BOJ June probability is 35–45%), while the strategic horizon encompasses 2–3 BOJ meetings where cumulative probability of at least one hike exceeds 70%.
cross_horizon_consistency.inflection_point: June 17 BOJ MPM: a hike would increase tactical conviction to 3–4; a dovish hold would reduce tactical conviction to 1 while preserving the strategic bearish view via July/September windows.
cross_horizon_consistency.trading_implication: Size strategic shorts at current levels with 117.50 stops; add to positions on BOJ hawkish delivery. Use 115.50–116.00 as re-entry points for tactical shorts in the near-term consolidation phase.

forward_vs_spot.forward_1m: ~114.19 (-38 pips, implying ~0.33% JPY appreciation vs CAD over 1M)
forward_vs_spot.forward_3m: ~113.43 (-114 pips, implying ~1.0% JPY appreciation vs CAD over 3M)
forward_vs_spot.forward_6m: ~112.07 (-250 pips, implying ~2.2% JPY appreciation vs CAD over 6M)

catalysts.rationale: If 4+ members express support for June hike or hawkish dissent rationale escalates, BOJ pricing shifts and CAD/JPY declines. The three-way dissent from the April 28 MPM creates scope for more aggressive language than expected. This is the earliest near-term signpost for the pair — the Summary of Opinions typically reveals the internal debate in more detail than the statement, and any escalation in hawkish language would boost June hike pricing from 35–40% toward 50%+, pressuring CAD/JPY lower by 100–200 pips.

A hot print (2.7%) reinforces BoC hawkish hold (CAD-positive, pair-bullish); a soft print (<2.3%) triggers dovish repricing (CAD-negative, pair-bearish). Energy base effects from carbon levy removal create upside risk. The bilateral impact is moderated by the JPY leg — Canadian CPI alone is unlikely to move CAD/JPY more than 50–80 pips in either direction unless the print is a significant outlier, as the BOJ-driven JPY leg has a stronger near-term signal.

Tokyo CPI above 2.0% would sharply boost June BOJ hike probability and pressure CAD/JPY lower. Consensus expects 1.5–1.7%; upside risk if energy subsidies wound down. This is the most important inflation data point in the tactical window — a Tokyo CPI surprise directly feeds into the BOJ's June decision framework, and a 2.0%+ print could shift June hike pricing from 35–40% to 55–65%, driving CAD/JPY 150–200 pips lower from current levels.

A weak print (<0.5% annualised) reinforces BoC dovish bias (CAD-negative, pair-bearish); a strong print supports hawkish hold (CAD-positive, pair-bullish). The bilateral impact on CAD/JPY is moderated by the JPY leg — GDP data affects CAD vs USD more directly, with the cross impact depending on whether the JPY leg is simultaneously driven by domestic Japanese factors. Consensus expects 0.8–1.2% annualised.

100% priced for hold at 2.25%. Statement language matters — any explicit hike signal or significant dovish pivot would affect CAD, but the impact on CAD/JPY is secondary to the BOJ June 17 decision one week later. The bilateral focus will be on whether Macklem characterises global uncertainty (USMCA, oil) as more or less constraining than prior statements, with any dovish shift providing a modest additional pair-bearish impulse.

Three April dissenters create 35–45% probability of a hike to 1.00%. A hike would shock CAD/JPY 150–250 pips lower in the immediate session, with follow-through over the subsequent week as carry trade positions are unwound. This is the single most important catalyst in the entire strategic window — it is the binary event that either confirms or delays the rate-convergence thesis. A hike would increase strategic conviction from 3 to 4/5 and likely trigger the 200d SMA break at 113.37.

catalysts.event: FOMC Rate Decision (same day as BOJ)
catalysts.rationale: Same-day as BOJ. Fed dovish lean compresses USD-JPY spread further; modest CAD/JPY impact via the vehicle-currency cross channel. The simultaneous event creates elevated vol across all JPY crosses and CAD pairs; the net impact on CAD/JPY depends on the BOJ outcome — if BOJ hikes, the FOMC becomes a secondary amplifier (Fed dovish + BOJ hawkish = double JPY-supportive). If BOJ holds, the FOMC outcome may provide a temporary relief rally for risk-on pairs including CAD/JPY.

75% probability of annual reviews outcome (mildly CAD-positive, pair-bullish on relief, ~50–100 pip upside); 15% withdrawal risk (sharply CAD-negative, pair-bearish, ~200–350 pip downside). The binary nature of this event means risk is asymmetrically skewed to the downside for CAD in the tail scenario. For the cross, the JPY leg remains independently driven by BOJ normalisation regardless of the USMCA outcome, moderating the bilateral impact relative to what USD/CAD would experience.

catalysts.event: BOJ MPM with Outlook Report — Critical
catalysts.rationale: If June was a hold, July becomes the primary hike candidate. Combined June/July hike probability ~70%. The Outlook Report allows BOJ to revise FY2026/27 CPI projections further upward if Shunto wage transmission continues. A July hike would be fully expected by this point if June held with hawkish framing, generating a 100–150 pip CAD/JPY move lower as the rate-convergence thesis advances to BOJ at 1.00% vs BoC at 2.25% (175bps remaining differential).

Candidate for second hike if July delivered first hike. Would push BOJ to 1.25% and compress bilateral spread to ~100bps. At this point, the carry-to-vol ratio would fall to approximately 0.105 — deeply unattractive — triggering systematic carry trade exit from long CAD/JPY positions. This meeting, if it delivers, would likely trigger the Phase 3 acceleration toward 108–111 and push consensus year-end forecasts lower. Currently not priced by markets.

invalidators.condition: WTI crude sustains above $120/bbl for 10+ consecutive trading days (Iran re-escalation scenario)
invalidators.mechanism: Oil's double-amplification effect (CAD export revenue surge + JPY import cost explosion) overwhelms rate convergence. At $120+ WTI, each $10 incremental move generates an estimated +200–300 pip CAD/JPY upside; sustained $120+ would push the pair toward 118–121, invalidating the bearish thesis entirely as the oil tailwind dwarfs the 150bps carry compression.

invalidators.condition: BOJ delivers dovish hold at June 17 MPM with 8-1 vote AND removes 'real rates significantly low' language from statement
invalidators.mechanism: The rate-convergence thesis delays by 3+ months, restoring carry attractiveness and removing the primary tactical bearish driver. A dovish hold with unambiguous communication would trigger JPY long unwind in CFTC positioning (56,400 contracts), pushing USD/JPY higher and CAD/JPY toward 116–117. The strategic bearish view would be preserved via July/September windows but conviction would fall to 1/5.
invalidators.monitor_indicator: BOJ June 17 statement language and vote count

invalidators.condition: CAD/JPY breaks above 117.50 (52-week high) with sustained 5-day close above
invalidators.new_stance: Neutral CAD/JPY (with upside risk to Bullish)
invalidators.mechanism: The trend regime flips bullish and the April 30 bearish engulfing is invalidated. A sustained break above the intervention-rejection level suggests either MOF tolerance of higher levels or a fundamental shift (oil spike, BOJ capitulation). CTA trend-following systems would flip to long, adding momentum-driven buying that could push the pair to 119–121.

invalidators.condition: USMCA review results in US tariff escalation above 50% on Canadian goods
invalidators.mechanism: CAD weakens independently of JPY on trade-war escalation, amplifying pair downside from both the direct CAD weakening channel and the risk-off JPY safe-haven bid that would accompany a major trade disruption. The CAD-specific shock would push USD/CAD above 1.50 and CAD/JPY toward 105–108 range, deepening the bearish thesis beyond the base case.
invalidators.monitor_indicator: USMCA review headlines and official announcements

invalidators.condition: Japan core CPI drops below 1.5% YoY for two consecutive monthly readings
invalidators.mechanism: BOJ normalisation decelerates and carry differential compression stalls. If Japan's inflation undershoots, the three April dissenters lose their hawkish rationale, June/July hike probabilities drop toward 10–15%, and the rate-convergence thesis is pushed into 2027. This removes the primary structural bearish driver, leaving the pair near fair value given current carry and oil conditions.
invalidators.monitor_indicator: Japan national CPI (monthly) and Tokyo CPI (leading indicator)

invalidators.condition: VIX spikes above 30 sustained for 5+ trading days AND Brent simultaneously drops below $85
invalidators.new_stance: Bearish CAD/JPY with increased conviction to 4/5
invalidators.mechanism: The double-compression risk scenario directly targets CAD/JPY from both legs: risk-off triggers JPY safe-haven buying (pair-bearish) while oil normalisation compresses the bilateral terms-of-trade advantage (also pair-bearish). This is the highest-conviction bearish scenario — both the carry trade and the commodity tailwind collapse simultaneously, potentially driving the pair toward 105–108 in a matter of weeks. This mirrors the August 2024 carry unwind anatomy.
invalidators.monitor_indicator: VIX daily close and Brent front-month daily close

signposts.indicator: BOJ Summary of Opinions / Board Member Speeches
signposts.current_level: Pending (~May 7–9 for April 28 Summary); April vote was 6-3 hawkish
signposts.confirms_if: 4+ members express explicit support for June hike; dissenter language escalates to 'immediate action warranted'
signposts.invalidates_if: Dissenters described as outliers; majority emphasises growth caution; consensus view frames June as premature
signposts.bilateral_importance: This is the most important near-term signpost for CAD/JPY specifically because it directly determines the JPY leg trajectory, which is the primary driver of the bilateral view. The JPY leg's BOJ normalisation thesis is more directionally clear than the CAD leg's USMCA/oil uncertainty — making BOJ communications disproportionately important for this pair vs other CAD crosses.

signposts.confirms_if: Drops below $90 sustainably (oil tailwind fades, bilateral terms-of-trade advantage collapses, pair falls)
signposts.invalidates_if: Sustains above $120 for 10+ days (double-amplification overwhelms rate convergence; pair becomes bullish)
signposts.bilateral_importance: CAD/JPY has the highest G10 bilateral oil sensitivity of any major pair — oil moves both legs in the same direction simultaneously (up = CAD strong + JPY weak = pair higher; down = CAD weak + JPY strong = pair lower). No other G10 cross has this double-amplification characteristic, making WTI the most unique and important external signpost for this specific pair.

signposts.confirms_if: Compresses below 90bps (JGB yields rising above 2.70% or Canadian yields falling below 3.40%)
signposts.invalidates_if: Widens above 140bps (carry attractiveness restored; JGB yields fall or Canada yields rise)
signposts.bilateral_importance: The Canada-Japan 10Y spread is the most direct market-based measure of the rate-convergence thesis that drives our bearish view. Unlike USD/CAD or USD/JPY which are each sensitive to USD dynamics, this bilateral spread is a pure measure of the BoC-BOJ policy divergence and JGB yield ascent — the twin engines of CAD/JPY downside over the strategic horizon.

signposts.confirms_if: Sustained close below 113.37 for 3+ consecutive days (trend regime flips bearish, systematic CTA selling engages, targets 110–111)
signposts.invalidates_if: Rebounds from 200d SMA and breaks 116.00 on a weekly closing basis (uptrend reasserts, bearish thesis invalidated)
signposts.bilateral_importance: The 200d SMA is the primary trigger for systematic trend-following fund engagement in CAD/JPY. A break below flips CTA positioning from long to short, adding momentum-driven selling that can accelerate the move by 150–300 pips beyond the fundamental-driven drift. This level is CAD/JPY-specific because of the pair's prominence in carry trade baskets and commodity-cross fund mandates.

signposts.indicator: CFTC JPY Net Leveraged Fund Position
signposts.current_level: +56,400 contracts (net long — unusually bullish JPY configuration)
signposts.confirms_if: Sustained net long in 40K–80K range (durable JPY-bullish accumulation consistent with BOJ normalisation thesis validation)
signposts.invalidates_if: Reverts to net short territory (JPY-bearish momentum returns; BOJ disappointment scenario) OR surges above +100K (overcrowded long, creates squeeze risk that would push CAD/JPY sharply higher)
signposts.bilateral_importance: The CFTC JPY net position is disproportionately important for CAD/JPY because the JPY leg is the more dynamic driver — the JPY long at +56,400 is an unusual configuration that reflects institutional conviction in BOJ normalisation. Monitoring this weekly tells us whether the thesis is being validated (position grows or holds) or rejected (position unwinds), providing a real-time sentiment indicator for the primary driver of CAD/JPY downside.

assumptions: CAD/JPY forward points estimated from the BoC-BOJ policy rate differential of +150bps annualised; precise forward outrights typically sourced from Bloomberg/Reuters terminals and were not directly retrieved from public sources for this analysis.

Pair-specific implied volatility estimated as a composite from USD/CAD (~7%) and USD/JPY (~9.5%) vol surfaces, adjusted for cross-correlation. Direct CAD/JPY IV readings not found from public sources; the 9–10% estimate is an approximation.
Risk reversal direction for CAD/JPY inferred from USD/JPY risk reversal shift (JPY-call premium post-intervention); direct CAD/JPY risk reversal data not found from public sources.

Weight adjustments reflect CAD/JPY's unique character as a high-beta, oil-sensitive, carry-trade pair where risk appetite and commodity factors have outsized influence relative to the standard G10 framework; Factor 3 tactical weight increased from 0.10 to 0.13, Factor 5 tactical from 0.06 to 0.09, Factor 10 tactical reduced from 0.11 to 0.05.
Consensus CAD/JPY forecasts sourced from Exchange Rates UK aggregated bank research, which may not reflect the most recent post-intervention analyst updates as of May 9, 2026.
Bilateral trade and investment data sourced from Government of Canada and PM press releases; specific Q1 2026 capital flow data not available from public sources.
BOJ hike probability of 35–45% for June 17 is our estimate based on the 6-3 vote composition; official OIS-derived probabilities may differ from this assessment.

The OECD PPP fair value of approximately 75–83 for CAD/JPY is derived from USD-based PPP estimates and should be treated as an approximation for a bilateral cross that is not directly computed by OECD.
